title: Script R10: Whole Metagenome Beta Diversity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"24lg6","text":"This protocol outlines the beta-diversity analysis that we performed on the whole metagenome samples. We measured the signifiance of the dissimilarity between samples that were grouped by biological occlusion status, microenvironment (sebaceous, moist, etc), and sampling time point. Here we also describe our intrapersonal vs interpersonal dissimilarity measurements. Based on methods from the following publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cr0h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6g39m","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4sgdo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c2pum","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

locale: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"11q6u","text":"## [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8pmv7","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"51ueq","text":"## attached base packages: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5rulv","text":"## [1] stats graphics grDevices utils datasets methods base ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"77gtd","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a71nf","text":"## loaded via a namespace (and not attached): ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"edrc2","text":"## [1] magrittr_1.5    formatR_1.2    tools_3.2.0    htmltools_0.2.6 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fdc3t","text":"## [5] yaml_2.1.13    stringi_0.4-1    rmarkdown_0.7    knitr_1.10.5 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"78a3d","text":"## [9] stringr_1.0.0    digest_0.6.8    evaluate_0.7","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: First load the required R packages. library(scatterplot3d)packageVersion( library(scatterplot3d) packageVersion("scatterplot3d") library(vegan) packageVersion("vegan") library(plyr) packageVersion("plyr") library(reshape2) packageVersion("reshape2") library(ggplot2) packageVersion("ggplot2") ## [1] '0.3.35' ## [1] '2.3.0' ## [1] '1.8.2' ## [1] '1.4.1' ## [1] '1.0.1'

Because there were many samples, and especially many OTUs, it took substantial computing power to get the distance matrix from the data set. We saved an R image of the environment that contained the distance matrix and stats. Because the R session image provides quick access to the data, we load that here for the notebook. The actual matrix can be found in the intermediate files . load('../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Beta_Div/distmatrix.RData')INPUT_MAP load('../../IntermediateOutput/Whole_Microbiome_Beta_Div/distmatrix.RData') INPUT_MAP <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Mapping_files/SkinMet_and_Virome_001_metadata.tsv", header=TRUE)

We will want to format the data for visualization, and then use NMDS ordination to visualize the clusters formed by our categories of interest. Generate subset of mapping file for only the specific anatomic sites and all time points (2 and 3). SUBSET_MAP SUBSET_MAP <- INPUT_MAP[-which(INPUT_MAP$NexteraXT_SampleID %in% NA), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[which(SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint %in% c(2,3)), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba","Ph","Vf","Neg")), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID %in% c(2,3,9,11)), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[c(order(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_SampleID)),]

Get only the samples described in the map subset. KEEP_SAMPLES KEEP_SAMPLES <- as.vector(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_SampleID) INPUT_SUBSET <- INPUT_NO_FINAL[which(INPUT_NO_FINAL$ContigID %in% c(KEEP_SAMPLES)), ] row.names(INPUT_SUBSET) <- INPUT_SUBSET[,1] INPUT_SUB_FORMAT <- INPUT_SUBSET[,-1]

Visualize the distance matrix using NMDS. BRAY_ORD_NMDS BRAY_ORD_NMDS <- metaMDS(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX,k=3) BRAY_ORD_FIT = data.frame(MDS1 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[,1], MDS2 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[,2], MDS3 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[,3]) BRAY_ORD_NMDS_STRESS <- BRAY_ORD_NMDS$stress BRAY_ORD_FIT$SampleID <- rownames(BRAY_ORD_FIT) NMDS_AND_MAP <- merge(BRAY_ORD_FIT, SUBSET_MAP, by.x="SampleID", by.y="NexteraXT_SampleID") ## Run 0 stress 0.1151716 ## Run 1 stress 0.1198662 ## Run 2 stress 0.1202445 ## Run 3 stress 0.1196748 ## Run 4 stress 0.1201702 ## Run 5 stress 0.1170017 ## Run 6 stress 0.117732 ## Run 7 stress 0.1226832 ## Run 8 stress 0.1211114 ## Run 9 stress 0.1173711 ## Run 10 stress 0.1162585 ## Run 11 stress 0.1188054 ## Run 12 stress 0.1163528 ## Run 13 stress 0.1180277 ## Run 14 stress 0.1191655 ## Run 15 stress 0.1199955 ## Run 16 stress 0.1193478 ## Run 17 stress 0.1241531 ## Run 18 stress 0.1182478 ## Run 19 stress 0.115657 ## ... procrustes: rmse 0.01284407 max resid 0.1230186 ## Run 20 stress 0.1176689

Now we will visualize the data as 3D scatter plots. NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories<-factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories) NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint<-factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint) NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion<-factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion) SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID<-factor(SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID) SUBSET_MAP$Site_Categories<-factor(SUBSET_MAP$Site_Categories) SUBSET_MAP$Site_Symbol<-factor(SUBSET_MAP$Site_Symbol) SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint<-factor(SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint) SUBSET_MAP$Occlusion<-factor(SUBSET_MAP$Occlusion)

Plot site microenvironment. s3d s3d<-scatterplot3d(NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS1,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS2,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS3, pch=16, color=as.integer(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories), type="h", main="metaMDS Ordination: Healthy Skin Metagenome Time Point 2 and 3") legend('right', pch = 16,legend = levels(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories)), col = seq_along(levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories)), inset=c(0.1,0)) adonis(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ SUBSET_MAP$Site_Categories, perm = 999, strata = SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID)

Plot time point. s3d s3d<-scatterplot3d(NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS1,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS2,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS3, pch=16, color=as.integer(NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint), type="h", main="TimePoint1 Vs TimePoint2 Bray Curtis") legend('right', pch = 16, legend = levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint), col = seq_along(levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint)),inset=c(0.1,0)) adonis(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint, perm = 999, strata = SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID)

Plot occlusion. s3d s3d<-scatterplot3d(NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS1,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS2,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS3, pch=16, color=as.integer(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion), type="h", main="Occlusion Status Bray Curtis") legend('right', pch = 16, legend = levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion), col = seq_along(levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion)),inset=c(0.1,0)) adonis(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ SUBSET_MAP$Occlusion, perm = 999, strata = SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID)

Plot occlusion with legend. s3d s3d<-scatterplot3d(NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS1,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS2,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS3, pch=16, color=as.integer(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion), type="h", main="Occlusion Status Bray Curtis") legend('right', pch = 16, legend = levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion), col = seq_along(levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion)), inset=c(0.1,0))

Get intra-personal and interpersonal distance similarities, showing intra over time is more similar than that site compared to all other sites. INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX was generated using the vegan command, vegdist (INPUT_SUB_FORMAT, method = "bray"). INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX It is loaded from the RData image to save computational time

Data frame reference: "sample tp2" \t "sample tp3" using merge function. MAP_TP2 MAP_TP2 <- SUBSET_MAP[c(SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint==2),c(1,5:9)] MAP_TP3 <- SUBSET_MAP[c(SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint==3),c(1,5:9)] MAP_MERGE_REF <- merge(MAP_TP2, MAP_TP3, by=c("SubjectID","Site_Symbol","Site_Categories","Occlusion")) SAMPLE_NAMES <- as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_SampleID.x) INTRAPERSONAL_DIST <- data.frame(lapply(SAMPLE_NAMES, function(i) { INTRAPERSON_DIST <- INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX[c(row.names(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX)==i), as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_SampleID.x==i,"NexteraXT_SampleID.y"])] SUBJECT <- MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_SampleID.x==i,"SubjectID"] SITE <- as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_SampleID.x==i,"Site_Symbol"]) RESULT <- data.frame(X=c(i, SUBJECT, SITE, INTRAPERSON_DIST)) return(RESULT) })) INTERPERSONAL_DIST_TP3 <- data.frame(lapply(SAMPLE_NAMES, function(i) { INTERPERSON_DIST_TP3 <- INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX[c(row.names(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX)==i), as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[-which(MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_SampleID.x %in% i),"NexteraXT_SampleID.y"])] SUBJECT <- MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_SampleID.x==i,"SubjectID"] SITE <- as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_SampleID.x==i,"Site_Symbol"]) TRANS <- data.frame(t(INTERPERSON_DIST_TP3)) RESULT <- data.frame(X=c(SUBJECT, SITE, INTERPERSON_DIST_TP3)) return(TRANS) })) INTERPERSONAL_DIST_TP2 <- data.frame(lapply(SAMPLE_NAMES, function(i) { INTERPERSON_DIST_T2 <- INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX[c(row.names(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX_MATRIX)==i), as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[-which(MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_SampleID.x %in% i),"NexteraXT_SampleID.x"])] SUBJECT <- MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_SampleID.x==i,"SubjectID"] SITE <- as.vector(MAP_MERGE_REF[MAP_MERGE_REF$NexteraXT_SampleID.x==i,"Site_Symbol"]) TRANS <- data.frame(t(INTERPERSON_DIST_T2)) RESULT <- data.frame(X=c(SUBJECT, SITE, INTERPERSON_DIST_T2)) return(TRANS) }))

Melt the two interpersonal distance data frames. INTER_TP2_MELT INTER_TP2_MELT <- melt(INTERPERSONAL_DIST_TP2) INTER_TP2_MELT$Type <- "Inter" INTER_TP3_MELT <- melt(INTERPERSONAL_DIST_TP3) ## No id variables; using all as measure variables

Get intrapersonal values in same format. INTER_TP3_MELT$Type INTER_TP3_MELT$Type <- "Inter" INTRA_TRANS <- data.frame(t(INTRAPERSONAL_DIST)) INTRA_TRANS_CUT <- INTRA_TRANS[,c("X1","X4")] INTRA_TRANS_CUT$Type <- "Intra" colnames(INTRA_TRANS_CUT) <- c("variable","value","Type") INTRA_TRANS_CUT$value <- as.numeric(as.character(INTRA_TRANS_CUT$value)) row.names(INTRA_TRANS_CUT) <- NULL
Bind together all of these data frames. BOUND_DIST BOUND_DIST <- rbind(INTRA_TRANS_CUT, INTER_TP2_MELT, INTER_TP3_MELT) BOUND_DIST <- BOUND_DIST[,c(2,3)]
Plot the resulting distances as means with stdev. BOUND_SUMMARY
Now we can visualize our results. ggplot(BOUND_SUMMARY, aes(x=Type, y=mean)) + theme_bw() + geom_bar(position=position_dodge(), stat=

Run a T-test to calculate the statistical significance of the differences in means. t.test(BOUND_DIST$value ~ BOUND_DIST$Type)t.test(BOUND_DIST$value ~ BOUND_DIST$Type)$p.value t.test(BOUND_DIST$value ~ BOUND_DIST$Type) t.test(BOUND_DIST$value ~ BOUND_DIST$Type)$p.value
creator: Geoffrey D Hannigan
